---
description: "Orchestrate surface checks: one parallel deterministic batch, then AI for the rest"
mode: "agent"
---

You are the **orchestrator** for surface checks on an emitter's generated
SDK — properties (naming, visibility/access, shape) the wire tests can't see.
You verify every check in **two parallel phases**, not one-by-one:

1. **Deterministic phase (parallel, no AI).** One batch call runs every check
   whose category has a routine — concurrently.
2. **AI phase (only the leftovers).** You verify the few checks with no
   deterministic routine; dispatch them in parallel if there are several.

Read:

- `packages/${input:emitter}/eng/scripts/client-criteria/context.md` — Emitter
  facts (language, generated-root, flavors, checks-doc path) and concept prose.
- The **checks doc** — the `checks-doc` Markdown table named in `context.md`
  (default `surface-checks.md`): the batch runner reads it for deterministic
  checks and you read its rows' `doc` prose for the AI ones. Use this path unless the caller
  explicitly overrides it.
- `packages/${input:emitter}/eng/scripts/client-criteria/verifiers.json` — the
  routing table, **authored once per emitter (by you or a human) — never
  generated by the emitter build.** A category listed here is decided without
  AI, either by a `check` (a declarative pattern the runner matches) or by a `test`
  (defer to an existing emitter test that already asserts this surface, e.g. a
  Java reflection test). A category not listed is verified by AI. Even with **no**
  verifiers.json at all you can still verify every check from `context.md` +
  the checks doc — verifiers.json only moves work from AI to code.

Inputs:

- Emitter: ${input:emitter:package folder, e.g. http-client-python}
- Flavor: ${input:flavor:one of the flavors in the emitter's context.md}
- Scenario: ${input:scenario:optional substring filter on the check id}

## Phase 1 — deterministic batch (parallel)

Run the shared runner **once**. It is a single language-agnostic tool
(`tsp-spector verify-surface-checks`) that runs every check whose category has a
verifier — in parallel, no AI — and prints `{"results": [...], "needs_ai": [...]}`:

```
npx tsp-spector verify-surface-checks \
  --checks packages/${input:emitter}/eng/scripts/client-criteria/<checks-doc from context.md> \
  --verifiers packages/${input:emitter}/eng/scripts/client-criteria/verifiers.json \
  --generated-root <generated-root from context.md> \
  --flavor ${input:flavor} --language <language from context.md>
```

Take `results` as-is — each is a verdict decided without AI: `how: deterministic`
(a declarative pattern matched the generated code), `how: test`,
`status: deferred` (a real emitter test already asserts this surface; the
`evidence` cites it), or `how: not-applicable`, `status: not-applicable` (the
emitter declared this category N/A for its language in `verifiers.json`). Do not
re-check or second-guess them.

## Phase 2 — AI verification (the residual, in parallel)

For each item in `needs_ai`, verify it yourself: find the target in the generated
package under `<generated-root>/<flavor>/` (folder matching the scenario), judge
against its `verify` text and context.md, and cite the exact file + line. Mark
`how: ai`. **If there are several, dispatch them as parallel subagents** (one per
item) rather than serially, then collect the verdicts.

## Phase 3 — merge

Combine `results` + your AI verdicts into one table, filtered by the scenario
input if given:

`id | category | result (PASS/FAIL/N/A/DEFERRED) | how (deterministic/test/ai) | evidence`

Render `status: deferred` rows as `DEFERRED` with `how: test` — they are covered
by the referenced test rather than checked here. Render `status: not-applicable`
rows as `N/A` with `how: not-applicable` — the category has no surface in this
language.

End with a summary: pass/fail counts, and how many were code vs test vs AI.

## Phase 4 — propose promotions (optional; only write when asked)

For every item you had to verify with **AI** in Phase 2, consider whether it
could be decided without AI next time, and if so propose a `verifiers.json`
entry for its category. Two forms:

- **`check`** — the emitter's `context.md` describes the concept concretely
  enough to be a mechanical file check. Propose a declarative pattern entry
  `{"<category>": {"files": ["…"], "find": "…{target}…", "expect": "…"}}`
  (placeholders/casing/expect as documented in `verifiers.json`).
- **`test`** — an existing test in the emitter repo already asserts this surface
  (e.g. a reflection/`assert`-based test). Propose
  `{"<category>": {"test": "<path/to/Test.ext#Lx-Ly>", "note": "…"}}` so the
  check defers to that test instead of AI.

Print the proposed JSON entries under a **"Suggested verifiers.json additions"**
heading with a one-line rationale each. verifiers.json is authored, rarely
changes, and is never generated by the emitter — so **only write the file if the
caller explicitly asks**; otherwise leave the suggestions for review. A verify
run is read-only by default.

The point: the bulk is decided without AI — a py routine or an existing test —
and AI is spent only on the checks that actually need judgment, shrinking further
as verifiers.json is filled in.
